name: Sarwar Uddin Ahmed, PhD
title: Professor & Dean
bio_raw: Professor Sarwar Uddin Ahmed is currently the Professor and Dean of the School of Business at ULAB. Prior to joining ULAB, he served as the founding Dean of Academic Affairs for the Monash and UoL-LSE programs at Universal College Bangladesh (UCB). Before that, he was the Dean and Professor of Finance at the School of Business and Entrepreneurship (SBE) at Independent University, Bangladesh (IUB). He has completed post-doctoral studies at the University of California, Berkeley (2018) and McNeese State University (2011-12), USA, as a two-time recipient of the Fulbright Scholar Award. Recently, he has successfully completed the Japan Foundation Fellowship on ESG Risk Management (2022-2023). Professor Ahmed has authored over 100 articles, books, book chapters, reports, and conference papers. His research has been published in various esteemed journals, including Corporate Social Responsibility and Environmental Management, Journal of Sustainable Finance & Investment, Cogent Economics & Finance, Sage Open, Global Business Review, and International Journal of Environment and Sustainable Development.
stated_interests: ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) risk management, climate finance, corporate social responsibility, corporate governance, capital market, behavioral finance.

Awards and Fellowships
Japan Foundation Fellowship (2022-23).
Most Outstanding Professor Award, World Education Forum (2020).
Fulbright Visiting Scholar (2018-19) United States Department of State, University of California, Berkeley.
Fulbright Scholar Award (2011-12) for post-doctoral study, United States Department of State.
Environmental Science Paper Award (2005), for the outstanding paper of 2005 awarded by the Center for Environmental Information Science (CEIS), Tokyo, Japan.
Nagasaki University President Award (2004) for serving as the founding General Secretary of the Nagasaki University Foreign Students Association (NUFSA).
Japanese Government (Monbukagakusho) Scholarship (2001-2004), for the PhD Program in Graduate School of Science and Technology, Nagasaki University, Japan.
Nagasaki University President Award (2001) for securing first place (Summa Cum Laude) Master of Economics (Finance), obtaining CGPA 4/4.
Audience Nominated Best Speaker Prize in the 10th Annual Japanese Language Speech Contest (1998).
Judges Selection Prize in the 10th Annual Japanese Language Speech Contest (1998)
IBBL  Award (1994) for outstanding result in the Master of commerce exam (Banking).
Jalal Ahmed Gold Medal (1993) for securing first class first position in the Bachelor of Commerce exam (Finance).
